spec_match.rationale: The demo now generates valid polygon() syntax for fill-rule alone and for fill-rule plus round, including the required comma before the point list.

interactive.rationale: Shape buttons, fill-rule tabs, radius and size sliders, a live 2x2 matrix, generated CSS, and parse-status cards all update from real controls.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page promises a fill-rule and rounding combinator and now also explains the ordinary-polygon cases where fill-rule should not visibly change the shape.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers true self-intersections, a bowtie crossing, concave and convex non-self-intersecting null cases, unsupported-round fallback, and invalid argument order.

references.rationale: The see-also section includes the direct chromestatus.com/feature/6636392944893952 link and an MDN polygon() reference.

design_system.rationale: The added UI uses existing CSS variables, keeps local button background and color paired, and remains an interactive demo rather than a static code card.

summary: The major reference violation is fixed, and the demo now better teaches the actual polygon() fill-rule contracts: valid argument order, true self-intersections, ordinary polygons where fill-rule has no effect, and graceful no-round fallback.
